title: Human Resources Intern
description:
Job Title: HR INTERN

About Us

“Capco, a Wipro company, is a global technology and management consulting firm. Awarded with Consultancy of the year in the British Bank Award and has been ranked Top 100 Best Companies for Women in India 2022 by Avtar & Seramount. With our presence across 32 cities across globe, we support 100+ clients across banking, financial and Energy sectors. We are recognized for our deep transformation execution and delivery.

WHY JOIN CAPCO?

You will work on engaging projects with the largest international and local banks, insurance companies, payment service providers and other key players in the industry. The projects that will transform the financial services industry.

MAKE AN IMPACT

Innovative thinking, delivery excellence and thought leadership to help our clients transform their business. Together with our clients and industry partners, we deliver disruptive work that is changing energy and financial services.

#BEYOURSELFATWORK

Capco has a tolerant, open culture that values diversity, inclusivity, and creativity.

CAREER ADVANCEMENT

With no forced hierarchy at Capco, everyone has the opportunity to grow as we grow, taking their career into their own hands.

DIVERSITY & INCLUSION

We believe that diversity of people and perspective gives us a competitive advantage.




We’re Hiring: HR Intern (Full-Time | Contract to Hire)

Are you a recent MBA (HR) graduate looking to kick-start your HR career with hands-on exposure to HR operations, documentation, and process excellence in a global environment? This opportunity is for you!

🔍 Role Overview

We are hiring one HR Intern to support our HR team in clean-up and validation of Employee Personnel Files (P-Files) across APAC regions ahead of an SAP migration, and to assist in revising, updating, and publishing HR Process SOPs.

This role is ideal for a detail-oriented fresher who enjoys working with documents, process improvements, and structured data.

📍 Location & Work Model

Location: EC, Bengaluru
Work Model: Hybrid – Office on Tuesday & Wednesday
Shift: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM IST
Weekly Off: Saturday & Sunday

⏳ Contract Details

Type: Intern (Full-Time)
Duration: 3 Months (Contract-to-Hire opportunity)
Notice Period: Immediate

✅ Key Responsibilities

Clean, organize, and validate Employee Personnel Files (P-Files) for APAC employees
Ensure documents meet compliance and audit standards
Assist in reviewing, updating, and formatting HR Process SOPs
Support SOP version control and publishing
Coordinate with the Hiring Manager/Coach for documentation accuracy
Maintain strict confidentiality of employee data

🎓 Eligibility & Skills

MBA / PGDM in HR (Fresher preferred | 0–6 months experience)
Strong MS Office skills (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
Excellent written and verbal communication
High attention to detail and process orientation
Willingness to work on documentation-heavy tasks




If you are keen to join us, you will be part of an organization that values your contributions, recognizes your potential, and provides ample opportunities for growth. For more information, visit www.capco.com. Follow us on Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn, and YouTube.

title: Senior Analyst - Assurance - National - ASU - FAAS - Financial&AccountingAdv - Bangalore
description:
Requisition Id: 1693496

As a global leader in assurance, tax, transaction and advisory services, we hire and develop the most passionate people in their field to help build a better working world. This starts with a culture that believes in giving you the training, opportunities and creative freedom. At EY, we don't just focus on who you are now, but who you can become. We believe that it’s your career and ‘It’s yours to build’ which means potential here is limitless and we'll provide you with motivating and fulfilling experiences throughout your career to help you on the path to becoming your best professional self.

The opportunity : Senior Analyst-National-Assurance-ASU - FAAS - Financial&AccountingAdv - Bangalore

National :

National comprises of sector agnostic teams working across industries for a well rounded experience.

ASU - FAAS - Financial&AccountingAdv :

Assurance’s purpose is to inspire confidence and trust to enable a complex world to work. We do so by protecting and serving the public interest, promoting transparency, supporting investor confidence and economic growth and nurturing talent to provide future business leaders . We help clients by :


Ensuring their accounts comply with the requisite audit standards
Providing a robust and clear perspective to audit committees and
Providing critical information for stakeholders.


Our Service Offerings include External Audit, Financial Accounting Advisory Services (FAAS) , IFRS & US GAAP conversion , IPO and other public offering, Corporate Treasury - IFRS 9 accounting & implementation support etc

Your Key Responsibilities

Technical Excellence


We are looking for a GenAI / Agentic AI Engineer to build and deploy LLM-powered applications such as RAG assistants, multi-agent workflows, anomaly/outlier detection accelerators, and conversational analytics. The role requires strong Python engineering, experience with LLM orchestration frameworks, and the ability to ship cloud-ready, secure, production-grade solutions. Key Responsibilities
Build LLM/GenAI applications (chat assistants, copilots, Q&A systems) using RAG over structured/unstructured enterprise data.
Develop agentic/multi-agent workflows for planning, validation, and orchestration (tool use, task decomposition, reviewer agents, etc).
Implement backend services and APIs (e.g., FastAPI/Node/Express) and integrate LLM APIs (OpenAI/Gemini/Azure OpenAI).
Design ingestion pipelines: document upload ? chunking ? embeddings ? indexing (vector DB / semantic search) ? retrieval + generation.
Add evaluation & guardrails: prompt discipline, relevance checks, grounded responses, monitoring, and feedback loops to reduce hallucinations.
Package and deploy solutions using Docker and cloud services (AWS/Azure/GCP/Vertex AI), with basic CI/CD practices where possible.
(Nice to have) Work on anomaly/outlier detection use cases in finance / risk / fraud / audit analytics and explainability Required Skills (Must-Have)
Python for AI application development and API services.
Hands-on with RAG and at least one orchestration framework such as LangChain / LangGraph (or equivalent).
Experience integrating LLM providers: OpenAI / Gemini / Azure OpenAI (prompting, tool use, structured outputs).
Backend/API development: FastAPI or Node.js/Express (REST APIs, auth basics).
Solid fundamentals: data structures, problem-solving, clean coding and version control (Git).


Skills And Attributes

To qualify for the role you must have

Qualification


Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science
Bachelor of Computer Applications
Certification in Data Analytics


Experience


Gen AI Fresher (0-1)


What We Look For

People with the ability to work in a collaborative manner to provide services across multiple client departments while following the commercial and legal requirements. You will need a practical approach to solving issues and complex problems with the ability to deliver insightful and practical solutions. We look for people who are agile, curious, mindful, and able to sustain positive energy, while being adaptable and creative in their approach.

What We Offer

Fuelled by the brilliance of our people, EY has emerged as the strongest brand and the most attractive employer in our field, with market-leading growth over competitors. Our people work side-by-side with market-leading entrepreneurs, game-changers, disruptors, and visionaries. As an organization, we are investing more time, technology, and money than ever before in skills and learning for our people. At EY, you will have a personalized Career Journey and also the chance to tap into the resources of our career frameworks to better know about your roles, skills, and opportunities.

EY is equally committed to being an inclusive employer, and we strive to achieve the right balance for our people—enabling us to deliver excellent client service while allowing our people to build their careers as well as focus on their wellbeing.

If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.

Join us in shaping the future with confidence. Apply Now

title: CA Qualified - Freshers
description:
Who are we?

Founded in 2014 by Khadim Batti and Vara Kumar, Whatfix is a leading global B2B SaaS provider and the largest pure-play enterprise digital adoption platform (DAP). Whatfix empowers companies to maximize the ROI of their digital investments across the application lifecycle, from ideation to training to the deployment of software. Driving user productivity, ensuring process compliance, and improving user experience of internal and customer-facing applications.

Spearheading the category with serial innovation and unmatched customer-centricity, Whatfix is the only DAP innovating beyond the category, positioning itself as a comprehensive suite for GenAI-powered digital adoption, analytics, and application simulation. Whatfix product suite consists of 3 products - DAP, Product Analytics, and Mirror. This product suite helps businesses accelerate ROI on digital investments by streamlining application deployment across its lifecycle.

Whatfix has seven offices across the US, India, UK, Germany, Singapore, and Australia and a presence across 40+ countries.

Customers: 700+ enterprise customers, including over 80 Fortune 500 companies such as Shell, Microsoft, Schneider Electric, and UPS Supply Chain Solutions.

Investors: Raised a total of ~$270 million. Most recently Series E round of $125 Million led by Warburg Pincus with participation from existing investor SoftBank Vision Fund 2. Other investors include Cisco Investments, Eight Roads Ventures (A division of Fidelity Investments), Dragoneer Investments, Peak XV Partners, and Stellaris Venture Partners.


Whatfix is among the “Top 50 Indian Software Companies” as per G2 Best Software Awards.
Recognized as a “Leader” in the digital adoption platforms (DAP) category for the past 4+ years by leading analyst firms like Gartner, Forrester, IDC, and Everest Group.
The only vendor recognized as a Customers’ Choice in the 2024 Gartner® Voice of the Customer for Digital Adoption Platforms has once again earned the Customers’ Choice distinction in 2025. We also boast a star rating of 4.6 on G2 Crowd, 4.5 on Gartner Peer Insights, and a high CSAT of 99.8%
Highest-Ranking DAP on 2023 Deloitte Technology Fast 500™ North America for Fourth Consecutive Year
Won the Silver for Stevie's Employer of the Year 2023 – Computer Software category and also recognized as Great Place to Work 2022-2023
Only DAP to be among the top 35% companies worldwide in sustainability excellence with EcoVadis Bronze Medal
On the G2 peer review platform, Whatfix has received 77 Leader badges across all market segments, including Small, Medium, and Enterprise, in 2024, among numerous other industry recognitions.


Be one of the key members in this awesome journey of building a billion-dollar company!

What will you get to do?


Liaise with Statutory and Internal Auditors and support the timely closure of audit processes.
Manage Transfer Pricing audit documentation and coordinate with external advisors for compliance and reporting.
Assist in preparation and filing of Income Tax Returns and Tax Audit Reports in coordination with external advisors.
Manage Accounts Receivable processes including invoicing, collections tracking, forecasting, and preparation of related reports.
Handle Intercompany transactions including agreements, accounting entries, invoicing, and related fund transfers.
Prepare and file statutory returns such as APR, FLA, and other regulatory filings.
Support GST compliance including return filings, refund claims, and related regulatory requirements with assistance from external advisors.
Review Sales Commission workings and plans in coordination with Sales and Marketing teams.
Support finance and compliance teams with any other ad hoc assignments as required.


What you should have?


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or Commerce along with a Qualified CA designation.
0–1 years of post-qualification experience (CA Fresher) in accounting or finance, preferably within multinational corporations (MNCs) or shared services environments.
CPA, or CMA (completed or semi-qualified ) will be an added advantage.
Familiarity with ERP systems such as Oracle, NetSuite, or similar platforms.
Strong proficiency in MS Excel, including pivot tables and advanced data handling.
Exposure to NetSuite would be beneficial for improving financial reporting processes.


Key Competencies:


High attention to detail with the ability to manage complex multi-entity reconciliations.
Strong communication skills to effectively collaborate with international teams and stakeholders.
Understanding of statutory and regulatory compliance across regions (e.g., social security in Germany, superannuation in Australia, CPF in Singapore).
Ability to prioritize tasks, manage deadlines, and drive process improvements.
Prior exposure to financial audits, including handling Transfer Pricing audit queries, would be advantageous.


Perks & Benefits (India)


Best-in-class medical insurance coverage
Free lunch & dinner buffet
Doorstep cab drop facility
Education sponsorship
Internal job transfer & global mobility programs
Scope to represent Whatfix at global events
We also provide uncapped incentives, bonus plans and opportunities to employees (especially those in GTM teams) to travel to meet our global customers


Note:


We strive to live and breathe our Cultural Principles and encourage employees to demonstrate some of these core values - Customer First; Empathy; Transparency; Fail Fast and scale Fast; No Hierarchies for Communication; Deep Dive and innovate; Trust, Do it as you own it;
We are an equal opportunity employer and value diverse people because of and not in spite of the differences. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status
At Whatfix, we deeply value collaboration, innovation, and human connection. We believe that being together in the office five days a week fosters open communication, strengthens our sense of community, and accelerates innovation—ultimately helping us achieve our goals more effectively. At the same time, we recognize the importance of flexibility in balancing personal and professional priorities. To support this, we offer the option to work from home up to two days per month.
In any situation that requires additional flexibility, we’re open to considering exceptions on a case-by-case basis to ensure you’re supported in managing both work and life seamlessly.

title: Delegates Sales/Acquisition Executive - Fresher Job in Bangalore
description:
As a Delegate's Sales/Acquisition Executive at Tradepass, you will have the exciting opportunity to jumpstart your career in sales while working with a dynamic and innovative team. We are looking for a fresher who possesses excellent sales skills, English proficiency, and a knack for client interaction and relationship building.

Key Responsibilities


Identify and acquire new clients for our events through strategic sales tactics and prospecting.
Develop and maintain strong relationships with existing clients to ensure repeat business and loyalty.
Understand client needs and provide tailored solutions to meet their specific requirements.
Effectively communicate the value propositions of our events to potential delegates.
Achieve sales targets and objectives set by the company through proactive selling techniques.
Collaborate with the marketing team to develop targeted campaigns and initiatives to drive delegate acquisition.
Provide exceptional customer service and support throughout the delegate acquisition process to ensure a positive experience.


If you are a self-motivated individual with a passion for sales and client relations, we want to hear from you. Join us at Tradepass and embark on a rewarding career in the events industry!

About Company: Tradepass provides elite business networking platforms across Asia, the Middle East and Africa, and Europe that connect the world of tech buyers with market intelligence, insightful use-cases, and innovative solutions from global experts, investors, and business partners. We believe in the relentless pursuit of breakthrough innovation, and the diversity of our backgrounds, knowledge, and experience to answer the various questions facing every business today - from cyber security, financial innovation, artificial intelligence, to new forms of smart data and digital transformation - all with a focus on 'transforming global businesses'.

title: Sales Intern/Fresher ( MBA only )
description:
About Us

Intellipaat is a leading global online professional training provider that creates courses in collaboration with top MNCs and universities such as IIT Madras, IIT Roorkee, IIT Guwahati, IBM, Microsoft, etc. With over 1 million learners and 200+ corporate clients across 55+ countries, we deliver industry-designed certification programs in Big Data, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, and 150+ trending technologies.

We help professionals make informed career decisions by offering expert trainers, hands-on projects, continuous evaluation, and industry-recognized certifications. We also support organizations in upskilling their workforce to stay aligned with evolving digital trends.




Key Responsibilities
Call leads from CRM and understand their upskilling needs
Recommend and pitch suitable courses based on requirements
Achieve revenue targets consistently
Identify cross-selling and up-selling opportunities
Generate referrals from existing customers
Maintain all interactions in CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce, Zoho)
Handle end-to-end pre-sales to post-sales activities
Follow up with leads and identify potential prospects
Gather customer feedback for product improvement




Eligibility Criteria
MBA pursuing students (graduating in 2026)
MBA graduates (passed out)




Preferred Skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Strong persuasion and negotiation ability
Ability to work under pressure
Team player with a proactive mindset
Fast learner with attention to detail




Work Details
Working Days: 6 days a week
Working Hours: 9 hours (including 1-hour break)




Compensation

For Both Interns and Full Time Employees:

First 6 Months (Internship/Training Period):

₹25,000/- fixed salary per month + performance-based incentives

Post Completion of 6 Months:

CTC will be revised to ₹8 LPA, comprising:

₹4.2 LPA Fixed

₹3.8 LPA Variable

Your first month’s salary will be on hold and it will be released along with your Fourth month salary, This is just for the retention purpose as per the company norms, will be same for both Interns and Full time employees.




Note:

No Agreement, Bond or Contract will be taken from Company side if you want to leave the company at any point of time you just need to serve the notice period and you can relieve from us.

Company will provide leaves only for Final Examinations not for Internals and No Preparatory Leaves.

It’s purely a Work from Office. No work from Home is provided.

Office Address : Arekere, Bannerghatta Road, Bangalore.

 

Candidates who are agreeing for the same can apply.

 

Our Location:6th Floor, Primeco Towers, Arekere Gate Junction, Bannerghatta main Road, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560076, India.

 

Feel free to contact us for any Queries : sushma.nelogal@intellipaat.com

title: Asset & Wealth Management - Ayco WSS - Analyst - Bengaluru
description:
Job Description

About Goldman Sachs

At Goldman Sachs, we connect people, capital and ideas to help solve problems for our clients. We are a leading global financial services firm providing investment banking, securities and investment management services to a substantial and diversified client base that includes corporations, financial institutions, governments and individuals. For us, it’s all about bringing together people who are curious, collaborative and have the drive to make things possible for our clients and communities.

YOUR IMPACT

Are you a quick-thinking self-starter with a passion for the financial markets, have the desire to work closely with high net worth individuals and the ability to think outside the box? Our client service professionals provide proactive financial counseling, full-service implementation, including tax preparation and investments, to address complexities of executives, entrepreneurs and high net worth individuals. We are seeking professionals with an understanding of the markets who have the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment where attention to detail, strong communication skills, and client service are essential to maintaining and enhancing our business.

OUR IMPACT

Wealth Management

Across Wealth Management, Goldman Sachs helps empower clients and customers around the world to reach their financial goals. Our advisor-led wealth management businesses provide financial planning, investment management, banking and comprehensive advice to a wide range of clients, including ultra-high net worth and high net worth individuals, as well as family offices, foundations and endowments, and corporations and their employees. Our consumer business provides digital solutions for customers to better spend, borrow, invest, and save. Across Wealth Management, our growth is driven by a relentless focus on our people, our clients and customers, and leading-edge technology, data and design.

Goldman Sachs Ayco

Goldman Sachs Ayco (Ayco) partners with Fortune 1000 companies to design and implement tailored financial planning programs for their workforce and leveraging the 50-year heritage of Ayco and the comprehensive resources of Goldman Sachs to meet the evolving needs of their employees. Ayco believes companies best serve their stakeholders and the greater economy when their employees financial lives are clear, understood and in their control. Ayco advisors and technical specialists help clients achieve their personal financial goals through education and guided implementation across seven key financial disciplines, including employee benefit planning.

Job Summary And Responsbilities


Serving as the primary operational driver of the Executive Wealth (EW) team by triaging incoming inquiries and tracking client priorities:
Asist Advisor teams, who work one-on-one with individual and/or corporate clients providing comprehensive personal financial planning, investment planning and tax services to corporate employees, executives and other high-net-worth individuals
Work closely with Wealth Advisor teams to support their AYCO clients
Leverage your operational background and experience to contribute to a growing business
Proactively anticipate, determine and recommend solutions to meet client needs and/or resolve issues surrounding investment related transactions
Manage cash balances and private banking services in accounts
Resolve time sensitive client service-related issues, including complex situations
Plan and manage new account onboarding process, asset transfers and other account service requests
Managing the pipeline of new business opportunities in Salesforce
Prepare various reports for clients in the areas of cash flow planning, income tax projections, investment allocation, investment performance, estate flowcharts, etc.
Facilitate various aspects of client portfolio performance reporting and analysis
Assist with tax activities, including tax return quality control, estimated payments, e-filing
Interface with internal groups to complete various investment and operational tasks.
Complete positive/negative consent client emails, handle exception tracking and comments.


Basic Qualifications


CA fresher Jan'26 cleared
Proficiency in Microsoft products such as Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook
Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality
Enthusiastic and positive approach to problem-solving and client service
Proven track record with cross-training, task-sharing and mutually supportive teamwork
Must be able to be innovative, pro-actively manage time and follow through to complete tasks
Demonstrated excellent verbal and written communication skills
Demonstrated ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment


About Goldman Sachs

At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world.

We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers.

We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability-statement.html

© The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., 2026. All rights reserved.

Goldman Sachs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, veterans status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

title: Wedding Planner
description:
About Meragi

Meragi is a rapidly growing start-up in India's thriving $50 billion wedding industry. As a technology-enabled wedding services provider, we revolutionize the way wedding-related services are bought and delivered. Our mission is to create pioneering industry-first solutions that address the complexities of this industry, utilizing innovative technology to consult, design, and sell wedding services with a strong focus on ensuring high-quality fulfillment. Backed by top-tier investors, we are at the forefront of transforming the wedding industry landscape. To drive our growth and success, we are seeking dynamic and growth-oriented individuals to design dream weddings for our customers.




Role overview

We’re looking for a design-oriented, people-loving fresher to join us as a Wedding Planner. This is a front-facing, hands-on role where you’ll be stationed at one of our wedding venues and own the end-to-end design and experience journey for couples getting married at that venue. If you come from a background in interior design, architecture, fashion design, or a related creative field, and love seeing your designs come alive in real spaces, this role is for you.




Roles and responsibilities

Welcome and host couples visiting the venue and conduct property tours
Help clients visualise their wedding and explain services clearly
Design and finalise décor concepts, layouts, menus, and packages
Coordinate with internal teams and vendors to ensure smooth alignment
Maintain clear communication and manage timelines effectively
Take ownership on the event day and ensure a seamless client experience




Who this role is for

Background in Interior Design, Architecture, Hospitality or similar creative fields
Strong visual sense and attention to detail
Comfortable talking to clients and presenting ideas
Calm, organised, and excited to work on live events
Willing to work on-site at a wedding venue




What you will get

Fixed CTC + uncapped incentives
Real-world exposure to design, client handling, and live events
Opportunity to see your ideas come to life at real weddings
Fast learning across design, hospitality, and project execution
Growth path into senior design, planning, or experience roles

YOUR IMPACT

Are you a quick-thinking self-starter with a passion for the financial markets, have the desire to work closely with high net worth individuals and the ability to think outside the box? Our client service professionals provide proactive financial counseling, full-service implementation, including tax preparation and investments, to address complexities of executives, entrepreneurs and high net worth individuals. We are seeking professionals with an understanding of the markets who have the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment where attention to detail, strong communication skills, and client service are essential to maintaining and enhancing our business.

OUR IMPACT

Wealth Management

Across Wealth Management, Goldman Sachs helps empower clients and customers around the world to reach their financial goals. Our advisor-led wealth management businesses provide financial planning, investment management, banking and comprehensive advice to a wide range of clients, including ultra-high net worth and high net worth individuals, as well as family offices, foundations and endowments, and corporations and their employees. Our consumer business provides digital solutions for customers to better spend, borrow, invest, and save. Across Wealth Management, our growth is driven by a relentless focus on our people, our clients and customers, and leading-edge technology, data and design.

Goldman Sachs Ayco

Goldman Sachs Ayco (Ayco) partners with Fortune 1000 companies to design and implement tailored financial planning programs for their workforce and leveraging the 50-year heritage of Ayco and the comprehensive resources of Goldman Sachs to meet the evolving needs of their employees. Ayco believes companies best serve their stakeholders and the greater economy when their employees financial lives are clear, understood and in their control. Ayco advisors and technical specialists help clients achieve their personal financial goals through education and guided implementation across seven key financial disciplines, including employee benefit planning.

Job Summary And Responsbilities


Serving as the primary operational driver of the Executive Wealth (EW) team by triaging incoming inquiries and tracking client priorities:
Asist Advisor teams, who work one-on-one with individual and/or corporate clients providing comprehensive personal financial planning, investment planning and tax services to corporate employees, executives and other high-net-worth individuals
Work closely with Wealth Advisor teams to support their AYCO clients
Leverage your operational background and experience to contribute to a growing business
Proactively anticipate, determine and recommend solutions to meet client needs and/or resolve issues surrounding investment related transactions
Manage cash balances and private banking services in accounts
Resolve time sensitive client service-related issues, including complex situations
Plan and manage new account onboarding process, asset transfers and other account service requests
Managing the pipeline of new business opportunities in Salesforce
Prepare various reports for clients in the areas of cash flow planning, income tax projections, investment allocation, investment performance, estate flowcharts, etc.
Facilitate various aspects of client portfolio performance reporting and analysis
Assist with tax activities, including tax return quality control, estimated payments, e-filing
Interface with internal groups to complete various investment and operational tasks.
Complete positive/negative consent client emails, handle exception tracking and comments.


Basic Qualifications


CA fresher Jan'26 cleared
Proficiency in Microsoft products such as Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook
Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality
Enthusiastic and positive approach to problem-solving and client service
Proven track record with cross-training, task-sharing and mutually supportive teamwork
Must be able to be innovative, pro-actively manage time and follow through to complete tasks
Demonstrated excellent verbal and written communication skills
Demonstrated ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment


About Goldman Sachs

At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world.

We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers.

We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability-statement.html

title: Sales Development Representative Intern
description:
About IQnext

We're revolutionizing the way buildings are managed, shifting them away from traditional facilities management to a smarter, data-driven approach. IQnext is an integrated, centralized platform that is disrupting buildings and real estate infrastructure with its new-age cloud-based solution, delivering long-term sustainability and operational efficiency that is scalable across portfolios.




Job Summary 

We’re looking for a proactive and driven Sales Development Representative (SDR) who will be the first point of contact in our sales process. This individual will focus on lead generation, qualifying potential customers, and booking appointments for our senior sales team. You'll play a key role in helping IQnext grow its presence in the enterprise and CRE market.




Designation - Sales Development Representative Intern

Experience - Fresher

Location - Bangalore

Opportunity: 

In this role, you will be responsible for driving new sales and the growth of IQnext in the enterprise segment. The person in this role will pioneer some of IQnext’s biggest deals by landing new logos and formalizing enterprise-wide relationships with companies. As a SDR, you will work with sales and leadership teams, to unlock new relationships at large organizations and evolve IQnext’s B2B revenue engine. You’ll also have support from our growing Sales, Product, Marketing, Customer Success, and Engineering teams to maximize the value and success of your efforts.

Career Growth at IQnext

title: Growth Marketing Associate (Entry-Level / Fresher Role)
description:
About Tacheon:




Founded in 2017, Tacheon is a growth-as-a-service consulting firm that helps brands with all things growth.




Tacheon has four core verticals:




1. Fractional Chief Growth Officer

We work with early-stage and growth-stage consumer brands as a Fractional Chief Growth Officer and drive all growth initiatives from process optimization to pricing, distribution and promotion strategies. These include product portfolio optimization, building processes and structure to scale, sharpening brand and product communication and efficiently scaling customer acquisition and retention.




2. Digital Transformation & Growth Strategy Consulting

We work with enterprise brands and help them build capability in their growth and marketing teams and solve specific growth challenges.




3. Data Warehousing and Analytics

We help businesses reduce their time from data to actionable insight by building a data warehouse with data from all business data sources, along with customized dashboards and alerts.




4. Generative AI Consulting

We have been working in the AI space since 2019 when we launched Smacient, a portal to help marketers leverage AI to be more productive and efficient. Today, Smacient has evolved into a solutions provider to help businesses leverage generative AI for business efficiency and growth. While we work on AI implementation at Smacient, we focus on AI consulting and advisory at Tacheon.




Why This Role is Perfect for Fresh Graduates:




Learn by doing: Get hands-on experience executing real growth marketing campaigns for both startups and established brands from day one
Master the fundamentals: Build a strong foundation in performance marketing, content marketing, analytics, and growth tactics before moving into strategic roles
Accelerated learning curve: Exposure to diverse industries, marketing channels, and growth tactics that would typically take years to accumulate
See the full picture: Work across the entire growth marketing funnel - from acquisition to retention - and understand how all the pieces fit together
Variety and impact: Work on multiple brands and campaigns simultaneously, gaining breadth of experience quickly




What You'll Do:




As a Growth Marketing Associate, you'll be the execution engine supporting growth initiatives for our partner brands. This is a hands-on, learn-by-doing role where you'll execute campaigns, analyze results, optimize performance, and gradually build the expertise needed to eventually develop growth strategies yourself.




Important: This is NOT a strategic advisory role. You won't be asked to "develop strategies" or "advise clients" - that comes with experience. Instead, you'll focus on executing the growth playbook, learning what works and why, and building your marketing fundamentals.




Core Responsibilities:




1. Marketing Operations

Work with agencies on performance marketing campaigns across channels (Meta, Google, Amazon, etc.) under senior guidance
Coordinate with agency partners to implement campaigns, track deliverables, and ensure timely execution
Create and manage campaign briefs, creative requests, and project timelines
Monitor campaign performance daily and flag issues or opportunities to the team
Help implement A/B tests and experiments designed by senior team members




2. Content & Creative Support

Support content creation for social media, email campaigns, and website copy
Review creative deliverables from agencies for brand alignment and quality
Coordinate influencer collaborations including outreach, negotiation support, and campaign tracking
Maintain content calendars and ensure consistent publishing across channels




3. Data Analysis & Reporting

Pull data from marketing tools (Google Analytics, Meta Ads Manager, Shopify, etc.) and create performance reports
Track KPIs including CAC, ROAS, conversion rates, and customer retention metrics
Build and maintain dashboards in Google Sheets/Excel to monitor campaign performance
Identify trends and patterns in data and share findings with the team
Learn to connect data insights to business outcomes and marketing optimizations




4. Research & Competitive Intelligence

Conduct market and competitor research to support campaign planning
Track competitor campaigns, promotions, and messaging across channels
Research new marketing tools, platforms, and emerging channels for potential testing
Compile research findings into actionable briefs and presentations




5. Project Coordination & Operations

Coordinate between internal teams, partner brands, and external agencies to keep projects on track
Schedule and prepare materials for client meetings and campaign reviews
Maintain organized documentation of campaigns, learnings, and best practices
Support product launch activities including pricing analysis and go-to-market planning




What We're Looking For:




Educational Background & Experience

Fresh graduate or 0-1 year of experience in marketing, digital marketing, or related fields
Internship or project experience in digital marketing, social media, or analytics is a plus but not required




Core Skills Required

Excel/Google Sheets proficiency: Must be comfortable with formulas, pivot tables, and data manipulation
Strong written communication: Ability to write clear, concise copy for emails, social posts, and reports
Basic analytical thinking: Comfortable working with numbers and drawing insights from data
Digital fluency: Familiar with social media platforms, e-commerce, and digital marketing concepts
Presentation skills: Can create clear, visual presentations in PowerPoint/Google Slides




Personal Attributes

Execution-oriented: You enjoy getting things done and take pride in delivering quality work on time
Detail-obsessed: You catch errors others miss and maintain high standards in your deliverables
Hungry to learn: You're genuinely curious about marketing, ask lots of questions, and seek feedback actively
Proactive problem-solver: When you encounter obstacles, you try to find solutions before escalating
Adaptable & resilient: Comfortable with changing priorities and learning from mistakes
Team player: Collaborative, receptive to feedback, and contributes positively to team culture




Bonus Points For

Familiarity with marketing tools like Google Analytics, Meta Ads Manager, etc.
Basic knowledge of paid advertising
Experience running personal social media accounts or small campaigns
Interest in D2C brands, e-commerce, or consumer marketing




What You'll Learn & Develop:




This role is designed to build comprehensive growth marketing fundamentals. Over your first 12-18 months, you'll develop expertise in:




Performance marketing: Hands-on experience with Meta Ads, Google Ads, Amazon Advertising, and other paid channels
Marketing analytics: How to measure, track, and optimize campaigns using data
Content & creative: What makes effective marketing copy, visuals, and messaging
Customer acquisition: The full funnel from awareness to conversion and what drives performance at each stage
Retention marketing: Email marketing, loyalty programs, and strategies to keep customers coming back
E-commerce operations: How online businesses work, from product launches to promotional planning
Marketing tools & platforms: Proficiency in industry-standard tools and analytics platforms
Campaign optimisation: How to test, learn, and improve marketing performance systematically




Important: This is an entry-level execution role designed specifically for fresh graduates or candidates with 0-1 year of experience. If you're looking for strategic or senior positions, this is not the right role.




We look forward to helping you build the foundation for a successful career in growth marketing!

title: Graduate
description:
Graduate & Experienced Graduate Program - Delhi Office




We are expanding our 2026 graduate programme and are currently seeking additional candidates to join the next intake.




Please note that if you have already applied to us this year, we kindly ask that you wait at least nine months before submitting a new application.







Our Firm

CF Corporation is a global leader in human capital and technology, with a track record of operating, investing, and advising since 1982. Today, the group spans 119 countries through a network of 30 companies, structured across four core pillars:

Human Capital: Delivering leadership advisory and talent management solutions that enable organisations to build and develop high-performing teams
Technology: Providing advanced digital capabilities across areas such as artificial intelligence, data, cybersecurity, and cloud infrastructure
Investments: Managing proprietary capital through our investment platform, deploying funds across public and private markets while working closely with our corporate finance arm
Philanthropy: Supporting global welfare and education initiatives for children, with a long-term ambition to become a fully sustainable, self-funded foundation










Our Graduate Program

CF Corporation’s London-based Graduate Programme is a structured, three-year development pathway designed to identify high-potential individuals and accelerate them into well-rounded, commercially effective professionals.




The programme is open to:

Final-year undergraduate students
Graduates with up to three years of professional experience at the time of application




Participants gain exposure across CF Corporation’s portfolio of 30 businesses, working across multiple industries and operating at the intersection of advisory, investment, and operational roles. From day one, you will contribute to live projects, working on real client engagements with tangible outcomes.




The programme offers flexibility in how you build your experience. You may choose to specialise within a single function over the full three years or rotate across three different functions, spending one year in each.




Available pathways include:

Finance & Accounting – Address complex financial challenges across a global portfolio of businesses
Philanthropy – Support and develop initiatives focused on education and welfare in Africa, including operational and strategic programme delivery
Private Equity – Source, evaluate, and execute investment opportunities using proprietary capital
Research & Business Analysis – Generate insights through data analysis, market research, and strategic evaluation, including equity research
Strategy & Leadership Consulting – Advise clients on strategic direction and leadership effectiveness
Human Capital – Deliver advisory and implementation projects across a broad range of HR and talent solutions
Digital Technologies – Work on commercial and technical solutions across key areas such as data, AI, and digital transformation
Investment Banking – Capital Markets Trading – Contribute to investment decisions across equities, fixed income, commodities, and currencies within a diversified portfolio
Investment Banking – Corporate Finance – Support M&A and capital raising transactions in collaboration with our corporate finance platform
Marketing, Sales & Commercial – Develop marketing strategies, drive revenue growth, and structure commercial solutions for clients




As a global firm, we hire graduates at one prime location as your stationed country. You then have the option to remain at your stationed country or relocate to any of our global locations throughout the program for up to three locations that includes your stationed country: https://thecfcorp.com/find-local-office/







Your Focus

You will analyse real business situations and contribute clear, practical solutions aligned to CF Corporation’s strategic priorities. This includes working through ambiguity, adapting quickly, learning fast, and delivering work that moves things forward.




You will work closely with experienced colleagues across the business, bringing fresh perspective where it adds value. From the outset, you’ll be involved in meaningful initiatives with genuine responsibility – developing your judgement, capability, and confidence by doing the work, not observing it.







Our Requirements

You can take in information quickly, make sense of it, and form a clear point of view. You approach problems with structure and judgement, and you are comfortable making decisions with imperfect data.




You bring pace, resilience, and follow-through, and you work effectively with others without needing excessive direction. You can hold your own in professional conversations, listen to alternative perspectives, and use them to sharpen thinking and outcomes rather than dilute them.

This role suits people who are curious, commercially minded, and prepared to be accountable for the quality of their work.




You must have completed your degree by the time you are part of our program.




We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

Data: We hire graduates throughout the year. Your initial step is to apply to our program with your LinkedIn profile. We do not require a CV or covering letter.




Feedback: Due to the sheer number of people that apply to us, we are unfortunately unable to provide feedback. Rest assured, we will review your application with care and you can reapply to us after at least 12 months has passed but not before this time. We believe this is sufficient time for an individual to show compelling change. Please apply to us directly via Linkedin and do not reach out to our people directly whether general questions or about this process. This is to ensure our process is streamlined through a single channel and ensure fairness for all that apply without bias.




Interview: We will invite you for an initial interview with our HR team, followed by a call with one of our Partners across our firm. We will not ask you technical questions, but we will better understand your capacity to think, motivation and life experience.




Subject to the initial interview round, the second round will require you to attend our one day event at one of our locations, where we have you be part of exercises involving other applicants. We look at how you interact with people, form solutions, manage ambiguity and work as a team for a common result. We run these rounds once per month and usually invite six people to attend at each of our global locations. Naturally, we will cover all expenses related to your process with us. Feedback is presented to you at the end of the exercise and potentially an unconditional offer.







Reward Structure

An annual base salary of 8.9 million and up to 15.2 million Indian Rupees, depending on years of experience and degree level (Bachelor, Master, PhD)
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 20% of base salary and up to 100% for high-achievers
Accelerated career progression over three years with up to 20 % annual increase in base salary per annum based upon personal performance
Hybrid working is embedded in the fabric of our firm. Depending upon your role, we offer work from home, office or hybrid elsewhere for a period of time.
Full healthcare including dental coverage for self and if you have an immediate family
Pension program with a non-contributory scheme of 12% of base salary per annum and options how your pension is invested with our preferred asset manager
Relocation is provided by the company for personal items and accommodation for the first six months upon joining
Opportunity to take one career break for up to six months and use this time for self or other
You will have a dedicate mentor for your career development pathway, built around real work, feedback, and exposure to senior leaders




This programme is designed for people who want pace, exposure, and accountability. Our reward program is structured for those that understand the level of performance required.







Learn More

Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for news and updates: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Read about our portfolio entities at: https://thecfcorp.com







Equal Opportunities

CF Corporation is an equal opportunity employer. We make hiring decisions based on capability, experience, and potential, and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other legally protected characteristic.




We value diverse perspectives and are committed to creating a fair, inclusive working environment where people are judged on the quality of their work and contribution.




Disclaimer

CF Corporation reserves the right to amend or withdraw this role at any time prior to appointment. By applying, you consent to the processing of your personal data in line with our data protection and privacy policies. CF Corporation will never request payment at any stage of the recruitment process

title: Tax Associate
description:
Company Description




VK TANEJA & ASSOCIATES is a reputable consulting firm based in Delhi specializing in Audit, Tax, and GST services. With a strong focus on analytical and systematic approaches, the firm delivers professional solutions tailored to client needs. Known for its commitment to excellence, VK TANEJA & ASSOCIATES serves a diverse clientele, ensuring compliance and accuracy in financial matters. Our expertise lies in providing strategic guidance to efficiently manage complex regulatory requirements.




1. ROLE OBJECTIVE

The Tax Associate shall be responsible for providing comprehensive taxation support to the firm's portfolio of clients across direct tax, indirect tax (GST), and regulatory compliance domains. The incumbent shall assist senior professionals in executing day-to-day compliance mandates, preparing statutory filings, conducting tax audits, and supporting representation matters before tax authorities.

2. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES & DUTIES

A. Income Tax Compliance

•    Preparation and filing of Income Tax Returns (ITRs) for individuals, HUFs, partnership firms, LLPs, and companies under the Income Tax Act, 1961.

•    Computation of advance tax liability on a quarterly basis and timely deposit in accordance with Section 208 of the Income-tax Act, 1961.

•    Preparation of tax audit reports under Section 44AB, including compilation of Form 3CA/3CB and Form 3CD with all required annexures.

•    Finalisation of books of account in coordination with the accounts team in preparation for statutory and tax audit.

•    Assistance in preparation of Tax Audit Paper Books and other documentation required for audit proceedings.

•    Preparation and filing of TDS/TCS returns (Form 24Q, 26Q, 27Q, 27EQ) and coordination on TDS reconciliation with Form 26AS / AIS / TIS.

•    Computation and assessment of MAT/AMT liability where applicable under Sections 115JB/115JC.

•    Drafting of notices responses, written submissions, and rectification applications under Sections 154, 139(5), 143(1), etc.

B. GST Compliance

•    Preparation and filing of monthly/quarterly GST returns — GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, GSTR-9, GSTR-9C — on the GST portal within prescribed timelines.

•    Reconciliation of GSTR-2A/2B with purchase registers for validation of Input Tax Credit (ITC) claims.

•    Computation of ITC eligibility and tracking of reversals under Section 17(5) of the CGST Act, 2017.

•    Assistance in preparation and submission of GST refund applications under Sections 54 and 55, including RFD-01, RFD-02 follow-ups, and deficiency memo responses.

•    Preparation of MSME-related GST cases and coordination with clients on documentation.

•    Drafting of replies to GST Show Cause Notices (SCNs), Assessment Orders, and Summons received by clients.

•    Monitoring compliance under e-invoicing provisions for eligible clients as per Notification No. 13/2020-CT and subsequent amendments — including IRN generation, QR code validation, and cancellation procedures.

C. Audit Planning & Finalisation

•    Assistance in planning statutory, tax, and internal audit engagements including preparation of audit programs and checklists.

•    Compilation and maintenance of audit working papers, schedules, and financial statement annexures.

•    Timely finalisation of audit reports and liaison with client management to ensure completion within agreed timelines.

•    Verification of books of account, trial balances, ledger scrutiny, and depreciation schedules.

•    Review of bank reconciliation statements and identification of unreconciled items.

D. Tax Planning & Advisory Support

•    Assistance in preparation of tax planning memos, advisory notes, and due diligence reports for clients.

•    Research on tax provisions, circulars, notifications, and case laws relevant to client queries.

•    Computation of deductions under Chapter VI-A (Sections 80C to 80U) and various exemptions for individual clients.

•    Preliminary analysis of transfer pricing documentation requirements and maintenance of Form 3CEB support.

3. ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA

Educational Qualification:

•    B.Com / M.Com / BBA / MBA (Finance) from a recognised university.

•    CA Intermediate (IPCC / New Scheme) cleared — preferred. CA Final students actively pursuing articleship completion are encouraged to apply.

•    Candidates holding a Certificate Course in GST from ICAI or equivalent body shall be given preference.

Experience:

•    0–3 years of relevant experience in a CA firm, tax consultancy, or corporate tax department.

•    Articleship experience in a tax/audit-focused CA firm shall be considered equivalent for fresher applicants.

Technical Skills & Knowledge:

•    Sound knowledge of Income Tax Act, 1961 and CGST Act, 2017 with their respective Rules and Notifications.

•    Working proficiency in Tally ERP 9 / Tally Prime, MS Excel (including pivot tables and VLOOKUP), and the Income Tax / GST portals.

•    Familiarity with e-filing portals, TRACES, MCA21 Portal, and MSME Samadhaan Portal.

•    Ability to draft clear and concise written communications, notices responses, and submissions.

4. DESIRED ATTRIBUTES

•    High degree of professional integrity, diligence, and attention to detail.

•    Ability to manage multiple client assignments simultaneously under time pressure.

•    Team-oriented approach with willingness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced practice environment.

•    Proactive attitude towards statutory deadlines and compliance calendars.

•    Strong command over written and spoken English; working knowledge of Hindi preferred.

title: Assistant Manager, Retail Business Development
description:
About the Company




Aditya Birla Lifestyle Brands Limited (ABLBL), a part of the Aditya Birla Group, is a leading branded apparel company with a comprehensive portfolio of marquee lifestyle brands.

As of March 31, 2025, the Company operates a retail footprint of over 4.6 million sq. ft., comprising 3,253 exclusive brand outlets, presence across 38,000+ multi-brand outlets, and over 7,000 shop-in-shops in department stores across the country.

The Company’s brand portfolio includes India’s most established and trusted names – Louis Philippe, Van Heusen, Allen Solly, and Peter England – each with a legacy of over 25 years.

These brands have consistently delivered sustained growth, driven by a sharp focus on design-led innovation, category expansion, and operational excellence across product lines spanning formal, casual, and occasion wear.

ABLBL has built a strong presence in the innerwear and athleisure market through Van Heusen Innerwear, which has rapidly scaled its distribution network across India. The Company’s portfolio is further enhanced through strategic partnerships with leading international brands including Simon Carter, American Eagle and Reebok. American Eagle has emerged as one of India’s most preferred premium denim brands, while Reebok – transitioned under the Company’s management in 2022 – has expanded its footprint across multiple channels. Simon Carter continues to strengthen its position in the premium menswear segment through its distinctive design language and curated collections.

Create Line of sight properties in Mall/ HS & convert them to store Launch as per budget & continuously look for newer opportunities for Conversion within the financial year.

Learn about the processes & systems with reference to RBD in particular and the organization in general.

title: Quality Assurance - Assistant Manager
description:
Your potential, unleashed




India’s impact on the global economy has increased at an exponential rate and Deloitte presents an opportunity to unleash and realise your potential amongst cutting edge leaders, and organisations shaping the future of the region, and indeed, the world beyond.




At Deloitte, your whole self to work, every day. Combine that with our drive to propel with purpose and you have the perfect playground to collaborate, innovate, grow, and make an impact that matters.




The team




The Information Technology department is responsible for building products and platforms for Deloitte India that focuses on providing extraordinary customer experience by putting design thinking with trailblazing technology in the center of what they do. The diverse team consists of subject matter experts, technology specialists, quality engineers, user experience researchers & designers, data scientists and product managers







Your work profile




As a Process Quality Assurance AM, you will play a vital role in ensuring the consistent quality and efficiency of our processes compliant with ISO 420001-2023.




Your primary responsibility will be Implementation of ISO 42001 framework across projects and functions and ensure compliance with AI risk, ethics, and governance requirements







Key Responsibilities




Implementation of ISO 42001 framework across projects and functions.
Define, document, and institutionalize AI governance policies, processes, and controls
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure compliance with AI risk, ethics, and governance requirements
Conduct thorough and systematic internal audits of various processes to ensure adherence to established quality standards and compliance with requirements.
Provide training and support to team members to ensure consistent understanding and application of quality
standards. Conduct awareness sessions and trainings on ISO 42001 and quality practices
Lead root cause analysis efforts to address process deviations, recommend corrective actions, and track the
effectiveness of solutions.
Participate in continuous improvement initiatives, driving the enhancement of processes to achieve higher levels of efficiency and quality.
Stay informed about industry trends, regulatory changes, and best practices related to process quality assurance.
Knowledge of standards like CMMI, ISO 270001, SOC and Statistical analysis will be an added advantage.




Experience




4‐6 years in Quality Assurance (IT industry)




Desired qualifications




Certificate - ISO 420001-2023 (Mandatory), CMMI, ISO 270001, SOC and Six Sigma (Added advantage)




Any Graduate with relevant experience. BTech and BE degree will be an added advantage
Proven track record of 4‐8 years in process quality assurance or a related role in IT industry.
Strong understanding of quality assurance methodologies, process improvement frameworks, and best practices.
Proficiency in using quality management tools and software.
Excellent analytical and problem‐solving skills, with the ability to translate data into actionable insights.
Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate with cross‐functional teams.
Detail‐oriented mindset and a commitment to maintaining high standards of quality.
Ability to adapt to a fast‐paced, evolving environment and manage multiple tasks simultaneously.




Location and way of working




Base location: Mumbai, Delhi
This profile does not involve extensive travel for work.
Hybrid is our default way of working. Each domain has customized the hybrid approach to their unique needs.




Your role as an Assistant Manager




We expect our people to embrace and live our purpose by challenging themselves to identify issues that are most important for our clients, our people, and for society.




In addition to living our purpose, Assistant Manager across our organization must strive to be:




Inspiring - Leading with integrity to build inclusion and motivation
Committed to creating purpose - Creating a sense of vision and purpose
Skilled at building diverse capability - Developing diverse capabilities for the future
Persuasive / Influencing - Persuading and influencing stakeholders
Collaborating - Partnering to build new solutions
Delivering value - Showing commercial acumen
Committed to expanding business - Leveraging new business opportunities
Analytical Acumen - Leveraging data to recommend impactful approach and solutions through the power of analysis and visualization
Effective communication – Must be well abled to have well-structured and well-articulated conversations to achieve win-win possibilities
Engagement Management / Delivery Excellence - Effectively managing engagement(s) to ensure timely and proactive execution as well as course correction for the success of engagement(s)
Managing change - Responding to changing environment with resilience
Managing Quality & Risk - Delivering high quality results and mitigating risks with utmost integrity and precision
Strategic Thinking & Problem Solving - Applying strategic mindset to solve business issues and complex problems
Tech Savvy - Leveraging ethical technology practices to deliver high impact for clients and for Deloitte




How you’ll grow




Connect for impact




Our exceptional team of professionals across the globe are solving some of the world’s most complex business problems, as well as directly supporting our communities, the planet, and each other. Know more in our Global Impact Report and our India Impact Report.




Empower to lead




You can be a leader irrespective of your career level. Our colleagues are characterised by their ability to inspire, support, and provide opportunities for people to deliver their best and grow both as professionals and human beings. Know more about Deloitte and our One Young World partnership.




Inclusion for all




At Deloitte, people are valued and respected for who they are and are trusted to add value to their clients, teams and communities in a way that reflects their own unique capabilities. Know more about everyday steps that you can take to be more inclusive. At Deloitte, we believe in the unique skills, attitude and potential each and every one of us brings to the table to make an impact that matters.




Drive your career




At Deloitte, you are encouraged to take ownership of your career. We recognise there is no one size fits all career path, and global, cross-business mobility and up / re-skilling are all within the range of possibilities to shape a unique and fulfilling career. Know more about Life at Deloitte.




Everyone’s welcome… entrust your happiness to us




Our workspaces and initiatives are geared towards your 360-degree happiness. This includes specific needs you may have in terms of accessibility, flexibility, safety and security, and caregiving. Here’s a glimpse of things that are in store for you.




Interview tips




We want job seekers exploring opportunities at Deloitte to feel prepared, confident and comfortable. To help you with your interview, we suggest that you do your research, know some background about the organisation and the business area you’re applying to. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte professionals.

title: Conversions Officer, Keele University Account at In-country
description:
Does this sound like you?




A recent graduate or a young professional with up to 0-1 years of experience.
Detail-oriented, organised, and proactive
Strong in verbal and written communication in English
Looking to gain experience in international education and admissions
Excited about the potential to grow into a student recruitment or conversion role




Position Overview




In-country is a British education management company launched in 2016 and now has a team of more than fifty people across Delhi, Mumbai, Lahore, and the UK. We manage the South Asian student recruitment operations for multiple blue-chip international universities and work on creative and exciting projects for many others. Our main office is located in Amar Colony, New Delhi.




In-country is looking to appoint a Conversions Officer to work as part of our dedicated team for our client, Keele University. As a Conversions Officer, you will play a vital role in guiding applicants through their enrollment process. Your primary responsibility will be to engage in conversion-focused outreach, addressing applicants' inquiries, and providing comprehensive guidance to ensure their successful transition to studying in the UK. You will keep students and stakeholders well-informed about academic program requirements, scholarships, enrollment restrictions, and other pertinent issues related to recruiting and admitting students.




More information on the University can be found at https://www.keele.ac.uk/, and more on In-country at www.in-country.com




Employment Type: full-time




Reporting to: Country Manager, Keele University Account at In-country




Location: In-country University Hub, E-265, Amar Colony, Lajpat Nagar - 4, New Delhi - 110024




Travel: Yes, domestic travel within India may be required occasionally




Key Responsibilities:




Lead conversion-focused outreach initiatives aimed at engaging Keele University applicants, ensuring timely and effective communication that addresses their queries, concerns, and motivations for choosing Keele University, ultimately increasing enrollment rates.
Deliver comprehensive and personalised guidance on academic program requirements, enrollment procedures, tuition fees, scholarships, accommodation, and other critical aspects of studying at the University, empowering applicants to make informed decisions.
Provide end-to-end support throughout the application journey, including assistance with document collection, understanding admission criteria, meeting application deadlines, and navigating technical processes to ensure a seamless and stress-free experience.
Liaise proactively with internal departments and the University’s admissions and academic teams to troubleshoot and resolve applicant concerns, clarify procedural doubts, and streamline the recruitment and enrollment workflow.
Maintain meticulous records of all applicant interactions, status updates, and follow-ups using internal CRM or student tracking systems, ensuring data integrity and enabling effective reporting and future outreach strategies.
Stay continuously informed about changes and updates to Keele University’s program offerings, entry requirements, and policy shifts, ensuring the most accurate and up-to-date information is communicated to prospective students and internal stakeholders.




Qualifications:




Bachelor's degree in any field.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
Excellent organisational skills and attention to detail.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Knowledge of higher education systems, especially related to international students, is a plus.
Previous experience in student recruitment or admissions coordination is desirable but not mandatory.




Benefits:




Extremely generous paid annual leave.
Competitive salary. 
Medical insurance.
Flexible working hours.
Super-fast professional development.
Emphasis on work-life balance, ownership of tasks, and staff development. 
Being part of a high-functioning, close-knit team.




How to apply: Click on Apply Now and fill out the form before 14 April 2026. Pro-tip: Write a Cover letter specific to this role.

description:

Work with the Mahindra Group, one of India’s most trusted brands.

title: Analyst - Business Consulting Risk - GOV - CNS - Risk - Risk Management - New Delhi
description:
Requisition Id: 1693288

As a global leader in assurance, tax, transaction and advisory services, we hire and develop the most passionate people in their field to help build a better working world. This starts with a culture that believes in giving you the training, opportunities and creative freedom. At EY, we don't just focus on who you are now, but who you can become. We believe that it’s your career and ‘It’s yours to build’ which means potential here is limitless and we'll provide you with motivating and fulfilling experiences throughout your career to help you on the path to becoming your best professional self.

The opportunity : Analyst-GOV-Business Consulting Risk-CNS - Risk - Risk Management - New Delhi

GOV :

Our Global Government & Public Sector (GPS) is a network of highly skilled professionals dedicated to serving our clients by bringing best-in-class international experience and insight combined with local knowledge to solve the most critical problems facing governments today. We actively focus on building solutions that help public sector entities face the challenges of the future and reinvent themselves.

Digital transformation is not just about new technologies; it involves an overhaul of organizational structures, governance, work processes, culture and mindset. It also means realizing a wider vision of relationships and business models that will redesign how public services function.

To achieve this vision, we help clients balance strategic choices to re-ignite performance. We have developed a suite of cross-service line strategic solutions that align with our client priorities.

We have a proud record of helping governments meet their challenges head-on, and we work closely with them to build a world that works better for all citizens.

CNS - Risk - Risk Management :

EY Consulting is building a better working world by transforming businesses through the power of people, technology and innovation. Our client-centric approach focuses on driving long-term value for our clients by solving their most strategic problems. EY Consulting is made up of three sub-service lines: Business Consulting (including Performance Improvement and Risk Consulting), Technology Consulting and People Advisory Services.

We help our clients in identifying and managing the interplay between upside and downside risk in order to make the long-term decisions to prevent risk and impact on their organization's ability to meet its future business strategy and objectives. The 3 key fields of play are :

Enterprise Risk - helps clients identify and address key risk areas, while building the agility to respond quickly. The areas of focus include Enterprise risk and resilience, Internal audit, Compliance and Internal controls

Technology Risk - helps clients to achieve sustainable growth by supporting their efforts to protect their business performance and by providing trusted communications on internal control and regulatory compliance to investors, management, regulators, customers and other stakeholders. EY teams accomplish this by assessing the technology risks that are introduced to businesses.

Financial Services Risk - helps clients in the financial sector to identify, manage and monitor the risks in their business. It is done through a transformative and agile approach including process and procedures, risk, data modeling and analytics, reporting to stakeholders and third parties, business requirements (for software selection and IT implementation), analysis of assets and liabilities, and capital and liquidity management.

Your Key Responsibilities

Technical Excellence


Demonstrated understanding of risk management frameworks and methodologies applicable to government and public sector organizations. Experience in conducting risk assessments and developing strategies to mitigate identified risks effectively. Proficient in analyzing and interpreting complex data sets to inform risk management decisions. Strong analytical skills in evaluating the effectiveness of existing risk controls and recommending enhancements. Ability to apply general consulting skills to deliver actionable insights and recommendations in risk management contexts.


Skills And Attributes

To qualify for the role you must have

Qualification


Post Graduate Diploma in Management
Master of Commerce
Master of Business Administration


Experience


Financial Analysis (3+ years)


What We Look For

People with the ability to work in a collaborative manner to provide services across multiple client departments while following the commercial and legal requirements. You will need a practical approach to solving issues and complex problems with the ability to deliver insightful and practical solutions. We look for people who are agile, curious, mindful, and able to sustain positive energy, while being adaptable and creative in their approach.

What We Offer

Fuelled by the brilliance of our people, EY has emerged as the strongest brand and the most attractive employer in our field, with market-leading growth over competitors. Our people work side-by-side with market-leading entrepreneurs, game-changers, disruptors, and visionaries. As an organization, we are investing more time, technology, and money than ever before in skills and learning for our people. At EY, you will have a personalized Career Journey and also the chance to tap into the resources of our career frameworks to better know about your roles, skills, and opportunities.

EY is equally committed to being an inclusive employer, and we strive to achieve the right balance for our people—enabling us to deliver excellent client service while allowing our people to build their careers as well as focus on their wellbeing.

If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.

Join us in shaping the future with confidence. Apply Now

title: CA Fresher- RM (Small Enterprise)
description:
Dear All,

We are pleased to invite applications from Chartered Accountant (CA) freshers for exciting opportunities in our team.

If you are a recent CA qualified professional looking to kickstart your career in a dynamic and growth-oriented environment, we would love to hear from you!




Job Title: Relationship Manager

Department: Small Enterprises (SE) - Working Capital Products

Experience: CA Freshers Only

Role Summary:

We are looking for a dynamic and result-oriented Relationship Manager (RM) to join our Small Enterprises (SE) team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for sourcing and managing a portfolio of clients with turnover up to ₹150 Cr maintaining portfolio hygiene and actively engaging in cross-selling financial products.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Client Acquisition (NTB): Source new-to-bank (NTB) clients in the small enterprises segment with turnover up to ₹150 Cr. Identify opportunities through market intelligence, references, and industry networking.

2. Portfolio Management: Manage and deepen relationships with the existing client portfolio. Regular engagement with clients to ensure satisfaction and retention.

3. Credit Note Preparation: Conduct financial analysis and prepare detailed credit notes for new and existing clients. Evaluate risk and structure proposals in line with banks credit policies.

4. Sanction Process & Stakeholder Coordination: Liaise with internal credit teams for proposal discussions and approvals. Effectively communicate with clients to understand their financial needs and ensure alignment with product offerings.

5. Portfolio Hygiene: Ensure timely renewals, limit compliance, documentation, and monitoring of early warning signals. Maintain high asset quality and minimize delinquencies.

6. Cross-Selling: Identify and capitalize on cross-selling opportunities across products like trade finance, treasury, cash management, insurance, and working capital solutions.

Desired Candidate Profile:

• CA Fresher

• Graduate/Postgraduate in Finance, Commerce, or a related field.

• Strong understanding of credit analysis and financial statements.

• Excellent relationship management and communication skills.

• Proven ability to drive business and manage risk effectively.
