title: Campus Recruiter
description:
Campus Recruiter will be responsible for building strong relationships with universities and colleges, managing campus hiring for various programs, and attracting top entry-level talent to meet organizational needs for Pune region. This role involves planning, executing, and optimizing campus recruitment strategies to ensure a strong talent pipeline.




Areas of Responsibility:

Campus Engagement:

Develop and maintain relationships with training and placement team, career development centre of institute, HOD and student organizations.
Design and execution of Pre & Post Offer Student Engagement strategy.
Strategize and contribute to effective branding initiatives at institutes within the region.
Representing the company at campus events, career fairs, and networking sessions.
Promote "TCS Campus Commune" as the leading social media learning platform.

Recruitment Process:

Understanding the hiring requirements from different units and coordinating with the CTAG to get the requirements fulfilled.
Manage end-to-end campus hiring, including branding and promotion at various channels of social media, planning the drive, interview closure, document validation and offer release.
Coordinate assessment centers, technical evaluations, publishing the dashboards with internal and external stakeholders.
Engaging BU leadership for driving niche skill hiring in the region
Organizing and Promoting participation for TCS Flagship contests like EngiNX, Codevita, EnQuode, HackQuest, Inframind, HumAIn etc.
Connect with senior leadership in the branch to ensure an effective pipeline of speakers at various events.
Staying updated about hiring trends and best practices and ensuring the process is being automated and made efficient with periodical intervals. Partnering with the team to design, refine, and implement innovative recruitment strategies and processes.
Work closely with managers to gain a comprehensive understanding of the company’s hiring needs for every role, and to meet competitive hiring goals and expectations.
Ensuring TAT and SLA are followed while submitting the reports, dashboards and while resolving queries for candidates or placement officers.

title: Investment Banking Analyst
description:
Investment Banking Analyst (Graduates and Experienced Hires) - Mumbai







Our Client

Our subsidiary CF Invest, is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on positions in the investment sector.




We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.




Our client is looking for two professionals to join their Investment Banking team in Mumbai as an Analyst and use their skills and ideas to make an impact on the success of the bank. You will either be placed in their FIG, TMT or Energy Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry including but not limited to Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications




Graduate – Analyst

Bachelor’s degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations
We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

Analyst annual base salary of 125,000 and up to 150,000 United States Dollars depending on experience. Your salary will be paid in Indian Rupees and bonus in USD
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
Relocation is provided by the company.

title: KYC Operations Junior Analyst
description:
Whether you’re at the start of your career or looking to discover your next adventure, your story begins here. At Citi, you’ll have the opportunity to expand your skills and make a difference at one of the world’s most global banks. We’re fully committed to supporting your growth and development from the start with extensive on-the-job training and exposure to senior leaders, as well as more traditional learning. You’ll also have the chance to give back and make a positive impact where we live and work through volunteerism.

Shape your Career with Citi

We’re currently looking for a high caliber professional to join our team as Officer, KYC Operations Junior Analyst Hybrid (Internal Job Title: KYC Operations Analyst 1 – C09) based in Mumbai, India. Being part of our team means that we’ll provide you with the resources to meet your unique needs, empower you to make healthy decision and manage your financial well-being to help plan for your future. For instance:


Citi provides programs and services for your physical and mental well-being including access to telehealth options, health advocates, confidential counseling and more. Coverage varies by country.
Citi provides access to an array of learning and development resources to help broaden and deepen your skills and knowledge as your career progresses.
We have a variety of programs that help employees balance their work and life.


The KYC Operations Analyst 1 is an entry-level position responsible for participating in Anti-Money Laundering (AML) monitoring, governance, oversight and regulatory reporting activities in coordination with the Compliance and Control team. The overall objective of this role is to assist in the development and management of a dedicated internal KYC (Know Your Client) program at Citi.

In this role, you’re expected to:


Partner with Relationship Management and Compliance teams to assist with the preparation, development, due diligence and approval of the electronic Know Your Client (KYC) record and supporting appendices
Create and maintain KYC records by obtaining information from internal and external sources (firm website, regulatory websites, etc.)
Partner with Relationship Management and Compliance teams to update system information from initiation to approval of KYC record and report workflow progress to supervisor
Validate the information within KYC records and Customer Identification Program (CIP) documents to ensure completion and accuracy
Ensure KYC records incorporate local regulatory requirements / Global Business Support Unit (BSU) Standards
Maintain BSU tool
Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency.


As a successful candidate, you’d ideally have the following skills and exposure:


Bachelor’s/University degree
At least 3-4 year related experience.
High attention to detail with the ability to interpret data and organize information
Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication
Demonstrated ability to remain unbiased in a diverse working environment


Take the next step in your career, apply for this role at Citi today

https://jobs.citi.com/dei

title: Admissions Officer, Keele University Account at In-country
description:
Does this sound like you?




A recent graduate or a young professional with up to 0-2 years of experience.
Detail-oriented, organised, and proactive
Strong in verbal and written communication in English
Looking to gain experience in international education and admissions
Excited about the potential to grow into a student recruitment or conversion role




About In-country

In-country is a British education management company launched in 2016 and now has a team of more than fifty people across Delhi, Mumbai, Lahore, and the UK. We manage student recruitment operations for multiple blue-chip international universities and work on creative, exciting projects for many others.

Our main office is located in Lajpat Nagar, New Delhi. More info at www.in-country.com




Position Overview

This is an exciting opportunity to join In-country’s growing India team as an Admissions Officer for Keele University. The selected candidates will support the admissions function and play a key role in application processing, student communication, and liaising with the UK-based Keele admissions team. This is a 6-month contract, with a possibility of extension into a permanent role in admissions or student recruitment/conversion, depending on performance and organisational needs.




Employement Type: Fixed-term (6 months), full-time




Location: In-country University Hub, E-265, Amar Colony, Lajpat Nagar-4, New Delhi - 110024




Reporting to: Admissions team, Keele University Account at In-country




Travel: Yes, domestic travel within India may be required occasionally




Main duties and responsibilities

Maintain public status as an employee of In-country India and not as an official of Keele University at all times
Evaluate and process admissions applications in accordance with university policies and eligibility guidelines
Communicate with applicants, offer-holders, and agents about program options, documentation requirements, and application status
Enter decisions and generate offers using the University’s Student Records System and other databases with high accuracy
Handle enquiries and provide timely, professional updates to students and stakeholders
Flag complex or exceptional cases, fraudulent applications, and policy-sensitive issues to the Account Manager or the UK admissions team
Support with evaluating qualifications and equivalency assessments in collaboration with the UK team
Assist in maintaining up-to-date communication records, managing the Keele India admissions inbox, and logging student/agent interactions
Conduct online or in-person sessions to guide applicants through the admissions process




Communication and Liaison

Roll out In-country’s agent and counsellor communication strategy for Keele University
Support the conversion team by contributing to offer-holder engagement activities
Work closely with internal stakeholders to align admissions timelines with Keele’s international recruitment goals
Participate in occasional outreach events to support application or conversion drives
Undertake research and provide insights to the UK admissions team on trends, bottlenecks, and applicant feedback




Qualifications and skills

Educated to degree level (or equivalent qualification/relevant professional experience)
Strong verbal and written communication skills in English
Excellent attention to detail and data handling accuracy
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with multiple priorities
Competent with Microsoft Office and data systems
Proactive, organised, and comfortable working both independently and within a team
Experience in higher education, admissions, or student services (preferred, not essential for entry-level roles)
Strong interpersonal skills and a professional attitude
Open to travel for training, events, or team coordination




Additional Info:

This is a 6-month contract role with potential to transition into a permanent position in admissions or recruitment/conversion
Salary will be commensurate with experience, starting from a competitive base




Benefits:

Extremely generous paid annual leave.
Competitive salary. 
Medical insurance.
Flexible working hours.
Super-fast professional development.
Emphasis on work-life balance, ownership of tasks, and staff development. 
Being part of a high-functioning, close-knit team.




How to apply: Click on 'ApplyNow' below and fill out the form before 14 April 2026. Pro-Tip - write a proper cover letter, bespoke to this job.

title: General Manager - Logistics
description:
Responsibilities

Operational Efficiency and Coordination:


Ensure achievement of targeted coal dispatches for the entire cluster.
Collaborate with HODs of Railway operations, Mining, Security, Civil, Electrical, IT, and Accounts departments for smooth logistics operations.
Manage operational Expense within approved budgets and monitor it on timely basis.
Implement safety protocols across logistics operations for the cluster.
Maintain regular interaction with the TPS team to ensure effective quantity and quality control.
Resolve any client inquiries (related to billing, inventory, sales, dispatch), and coordinate with relevant departments to address and resolve any issues.
Oversee accurate and timely data entry in MIS systems, maintaining proper records for future reference


Railway Liaison And Representation


Liaise with Railway at the Division level to ensure rake availability and address critical issues.
Monitor and facilitate timely EDRM sanctions to support operational needs.


Financial Management And Reporting


Achieve annual budgetary targets and oversee procurement processes (PRs, SES, NIS).
Ensure timely vendor invoicing and oversee MIS reporting.


Digitisation And Automation


Utilize digital platforms for real-time data analysis, communication, and project management to enhance cluster-level logistics performance and efficiency.
Leverage digital tools to identify inefficiencies, drive process improvements, and ensure seamless logistics operations across the cluster.


People And Team Engagement


Lead, inspire and mentor the team to foster a high-performance culture.
Drive and enhance employee engagement across the team to sustain a positive workplace culture.
Foster a conducive environment for high employee retention by ensuring team satisfaction and professional development.
Supervise and support team to achieve operational goals.
Identify and develop successors for smooth functioning of business operations.

Railway operations

Mining

Security

Civil Engineering

IT

Finance

HR Department

Railway Authorities

Vendors and Suppliers

Regulatory Authorities

Contractors

Third-Party Inspection Agencies

Qualifications

Master's degree in Logistics or Supply Chain Management preferred.

Work Experience

15 years of work experience in logistics or supply chain management, with cluster or regional experience
